text.title: SSL/TLS Management: Examples
text.summary: NERO platform Cloudflare origin cert setup (15-year, no renewal), Let's Encrypt direct domain, and troubleshooting the Flexible mode redirect loop.

section.title: Cloudflare Origin Cert Setup (NERO)
section.p: All NERO platform domains use Cloudflare Full(Strict) with a 15-year origin cert. Zero renewal ops.

section.title: Let's Encrypt Direct Domain
section.p: For a domain not behind Cloudflare (gray cloud / DNS-only).

example.code:
sudo apt install -y certbot python3-certbot-nginx
# Issue certificate (nginx plugin auto-configures nginx) sudo certbot --nginx -d example.com -d www.example.com
# Test renewal sudo certbot renew --dry-run
# Post-renewal hook to reload nginx sudo mkdir -p /etc/letsencrypt/renewal-hooks/post sudo tee /etc/letsencrypt/renewal-hooks/post/reload-nginx.sh << 'HOOK' #!/bin/bash systemctl reload nginx HOOK sudo chmod +x /etc/letsencrypt/renewal-hooks/post/reload-nginx.sh
# Symptom: ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS # Cloudflare SSL mode: Flexible # nginx forces HTTPS: return 301 https://... # # Cloudflare → origin HTTP → nginx redirects to HTTPS → Cloudflare sends HTTP → loop
# Fix: Change Cloudflare SSL mode to Full (Strict) # Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Full (Strict) # Then place a valid origin cert on the server.

# CLI certificate check echo | openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates -subject
# Test TLS version support openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 -tls1_3 # should succeed openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 -tls1 # should fail
# Check security headers curl -sI https://example.com | grep -iE "strict-transport|x-content-type|x-frame"

see-also.link: Cert expiry check for all configured domains
